claudeMd:
# Project Context

You are completing the dynamic terminal resize feature for MogTerm, a browser-based terminal emulator.

## What's Already Done

The `src/renderer.js` already has full resize support:
- `_measureCellSize()` measures char cell dimensions from rendered font
- `_setupResizeObserver()` attaches ResizeObserver with 100ms debounce
- `_handleResize()` computes cols/rows from pixel dimensions, calls `terminal.resize()`, re-renders, fires `onResize` callback
- `onResize` callback property for PTY/backend notification
- `dispose()` cleanup method

Do NOT modify `src/renderer.js`, `src/terminal.js`, or `src/parser.js`.

## What You Need to Do

Update `demo/index.html` only:

### 1. Make terminal container resizable
Add CSS to `#terminal-container`:
```css
resize: both;
overflow: hidden;
width: 680px;
height: 420px;
```
Remove `width: fit-content` (conflicts with resize).

### 2. Add status indicator
Add a `<div id="resize-status">` element between the terminal container and controls, styled to show current terminal dimensions (e.g., "80 cols x 24 rows"). Style it subtly (small font, muted color).

### 3. Hook onResize callback
In the `<script>` section, after creating the renderer:
```js
const statusEl = document.getElementById('resize-status');
statusEl.textContent = `${term.cols} cols x ${term.rows} rows`;
renderer.onResize = (cols, rows) => {
  statusEl.textContent = `${cols} cols x ${rows} rows`;
};
```

## Conventions
- ES modules (import/export)
- No build step — files loaded directly as ES modules
- Font: 'Courier New', Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', monospace at 14px, lineHeight 1.2
- Container padding: 8px
- Dark theme: bg #111, text #ccc, accent #7af

## Testing
- Run `node test/terminal.test.js` to verify all 119 tests still pass
- The demo should show a resize handle on the terminal container corner
- Dragging the handle should update the terminal dimensions and status display
- All existing demo buttons (Color Demo, ASCII Art, etc.) must still work after resize

## Gotchas
- The container must have explicit width/height for CSS `resize` to work
- `width: fit-content` must be removed as it conflicts with manual resizing
- Keep `overflow: hidden` on the container (already set by renderer._setup())
- Don't add DOM-dependent tests to terminal.test.js (runs in Node.js)

checks: The demo page at /demo/index.html shows a terminal that can be resized by dragging its corner/edge (CSS resize handle visible)
When the terminal container is resized by dragging, the terminal content re-renders to fill the new dimensions with no visual artifacts or clipping
A status indicator or log on the page shows the current terminal dimensions (cols x rows) updating in real time as the container is resized
The resize does not fire excessively — there is a visible debounce (content updates ~100ms after drag stops, not on every pixel change)
Existing demo functionality (Color Demo, ASCII Art, Cursor Movement, Text Attributes, Progress Bar, Reset buttons) continues to work correctly after resize
The terminal text uses the correct monospace font and character cells are aligned properly after resize
